01WHAT IT IS
Nation's leading garage door repair/install franchise, Neighborly/FirstService Brands. 132 US units (Dec 31, 2024), all franchised.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $164-360K. Item 7 $164,285-$360,294. Franchise fee $75K-$150K (population-based). Service trucks + wraps, door/spring inventory, specialized tools, dispatch software, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
102 units (2021) → 111 (2023) → 132 (2024). Growing steadily. Note multiple PE franchisee roll-ups (Franchise Equity Partners, SBJ Capital) acquiring territories. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 among the strongest in home services - the 2024 FDD (2023 data, 103 reporting franchised businesses) reports system average gross sales of $5,684,906 (Vetted Biz cites $4,746,462; 2022 average was $5.2M). Garage-door net ~12-18% → six-figure to $500K+ owner earnings at scale. Payback ~18-34 months.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

The Gutter Guys

Garage / Gutter / Fence / Floors · Vol II CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$15K

~$92K

~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $28K; liquid $28K
01WHAT IT IS
Seamless gutters; $15K fee - lowest-gate home-service option.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $15K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around ~$92K. The buy-in fee alone is $15K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Fire/water/mold/biohazard restoration franchise ("Paramedics of Property Damage"). 500+ US/NA units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $226-262K. Item 7 ~$86K-$220K (lower entry than SERVPRO/Paul Davis). Franchise fee $55K. Restoration equipment, van/truck, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
500+ locations; sales rose 5.3% to $478M (2024). Growing. High franchisee satisfaction (internal surveys >90% in 2024). Green flag. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 - 387 reporting franchisees averaged $953,564; under 2 yrs ~$397,072; 3-6 yrs ~$832,402; 7+ yrs ~$1,420,870. Restoration net ~13-20%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?

$40K
All-in cost
$82-195K
Royalty+ad
5% + 2%
Net worth req
$250K
Liquid req
$50K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH n/a LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: liquid $50K
01WHAT IT IS
Residential/commercial painting franchise, Neighborly. ~234-255 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $82-195K. Item 7 ~$82,200-$194,600 (2026). Franchise fee $45K; 6% royalty + 2% MAP. Low physical cost - estimating software, marketing, one vehicle, subcontractor crews, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~225 (2021) → 234 (2023) → 255+ (2026 claim). Flat-to-modest growth. Neighborly acquired it in 2015 at 77 locations. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average annual sales ~$592,979 (per Franchise Times); Vetted Biz cites ~$572,580. The 2024 FDD frames FPR around per-capita gross sales rather than a single AUV. Painting net ~8-15% (subcontractor/labor heavy).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Neighborly/portfolio-style manager-run model; owning other non-competing brands is common. In-term non-compete covers competing services only.

01WHAT IT IS
Residential/commercial painting franchise, Strategic Franchising Systems. ~174 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $45K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $86-125K. Item 7 $81,150-$120,250. Franchise fee up to $49,900; 6%→4% royalty + 2% branding. Estimating/CRM software, marketing, vehicle, subcontractors, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~174-180 units; stable. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 median gross revenue ~$533,010 (2023 data, 73 franchisees); Vetted Biz cites ~$594,928. Painting net ~8-15%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

The Painting Pros

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID ~
Known gate figures: NW req $100K; liquid $28-108K
01WHAT IT IS
Painting; Home Depot relationship for credibility.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $15K+. The buy-in fee alone is $30K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Handyman and small-repair franchise (Neighborly) with ~340 locations.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $143-180K. Item 7 $123,000-$159,600. 7% license fee + 2% MAP. Vans/wraps per craftsman, tools, dispatch software, insurance, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
347 US units; 151 franchisees operating 310 businesses in 2024 Item 19. Growing. Owners don't do the work. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2024 data) ~$890K average sales (2023 similar); 70%+ revenue from repeat customers. Handyman net ~15-22% at 5-7 trucks; mature multi-truck units reach $1M-$2M.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Neighborly/portfolio-style manager-run model; owning other non-competing brands is common. In-term non-compete covers competing services only.

01WHAT IT IS
Handyman franchise owned by Ace Hardware Corp (since 2019); ~4,800 Ace stores as referral partners. 369 franchised territories (Dec 31, 2024).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around ~$110-165K. Item 7 $131,997-$223,797. Franchise fee $70K (up to 100K households); 6% royalty + 2% brand fee; $30K-$50K minimum annual marketing. Vans, tools, office, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
369 franchised + 18 affiliate territories (214 owners) end of 2024. Growing post-Ace acquisition. Note: 33 territories closed at some point in 2024 (churn to watch). Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average revenue ~$699K (18-unit sample) to ~$501K (2023 all-single-territory); top 25% avg gross profit ~$224K. Handyman net ~15-22%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $120-157K. Item 7 $171,524-$223,965. Franchise fee $59,500; tiered royalty from 6-8.5%. One sales vehicle, drone/tech tools, subcontractor installers (low overhead - no office/heavy equipment needed).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Explosive growth - 242 franchised units (2022) → 388 (2024); 75 new units in 2024. RED FLAG: ongoing litigation against the franchisor's affiliates/executives alleging fraudulent misrepresentation and breach of contract. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2022 data) system avg ~$1.29M; top 25% (2024) avg ~$3.57M; avg job size ~$12,012. Roofing net ~10-20%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Manager-run roofing model; own other non-competing brands freely.
CASE FILE 103▶

Honest Abe Roofing

Painting / Handyman / Roofing · Vol II
Buy-in fee
est. $25-50K
All-in cost
$185-466K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
Known gate figures: NW req $300K; liquid $150K
01WHAT IT IS
Roofing; income prerequisites ($150K/yr x 5 yrs).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $185-466K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Known gate figures: NW req $300K
01WHAT IT IS
Eco-friendly steam mobile car/truck detailing. Chattanooga; founded 2005. ~9-12 franchised.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $81-195K. Total investment $88,500-$195,100; franchise fee $35,000; service vehicle(s), steam/detailing equipment, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Small system - thin data; FDD discloses lawsuits/bankruptcy. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
No standard Item 19; third-party gross estimate ~$130K; mobile detailing nets ~15-25%, labor-dependent. Royalty 6% + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Express/full-service car wash + detailing. Small regional franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around varies. Multi-million: land, tunnel, wash equipment.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Small/regional - thin data. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Limited public Item 19; express-wash economics - high fixed cost, membership-driven, strong net at volume.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Semi-absentee / manager-run, and the non-compete covers competing businesses only - it slots cleanly into a multi-brand portfolio.

01WHAT IT IS

Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.

All-in cost lands around $3.5-6M. Total investment $4.98M-$8.52M per site - land, tunnel construction, conveyor/wash equipment, water reclamation, vacuums, signage; franchise fee $50,000; SBA loans average ~$3.53M.

Rapid growth (No. 35 on 2026 Franchise 500); FDD discloses lawsuits/bankruptcy - review. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): average gross sales ~$1.65M/site (mature sites higher); strong net after fixed costs thanks to low labor and recurring memberships, but expect 3-5 year capital recovery. Royalty 4% + 1% brand fund.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.

Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.

All-in cost lands around $300-815K. 1,200-1,900 sq ft; total investment $275,500-$814,500 (+$40K-$60K for drive-thru). Blenders, prep line, flatbread oven, POS, signage. Franchise fee $35K.

Doubled footprint in ~5 years to 1,500+; 12+ consecutive years positive same-store sales; ~70% of 2024 openings from existing franchisees. Green flags. Watch: sugar/FDA "healthy"-labeling scrutiny. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.

System AUV $1,005,063; top-50% $1,282,144 (2024 FDD); bottom 50% ~$732K. Fast-casual ~13.5% net cited; realistic ~$115K-$143K. Royalty 6% + 5% ad.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

Flavored-crust pizza + oven-baked subs; ~515 units (MI-based); parent Hungry Howie's Pizza & Subs, Inc.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $292-567K. Total investment $431,873-$629,908 (incl. $79K-$94K to franchisor). Ovens, cooler, prep line, POS, delivery, signage. Franchise fee $12.5K-$25K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~515 units, stable/modest. No major red flags. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Avg gross sales ~$812,000-$860,000 (published). Pizza QSR nets 10-15% top / thinner average. Royalty 5.5%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 115▶

Dickey's Barbecue Pit

Food & Beverage · Vol II
Buy-in fee
est. $25-50K
All-in cost
$210-529K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
$450K
Liquid req
$100K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $450K; liquid $100K
01WHAT IT IS
Texas-style barbecue fast-casual franchise; ~450 locations.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $210-529K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-operator BBQ QSR; own other non-competing brands freely.

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $692K-1.52M. Drive-thru kiosk build $794,000-$1,341,500 (endcap lower). Espresso machines, drive-thru tech, POS, signage. Franchise fee $40K. Requires $500K net worth / $200K liquid.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~83 stores added in 2025 (~10% net unit growth); 2nd-largest US drive-thru coffee behind Dutch Bros; 0.0% SBA default rate cited. Strong green flags. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
System AUV ~$880,000-$885,355; top-quartile ~$1.27M (FDD Item 19); franchisor cites ~26% net margin at higher volumes. Realistic ~$150K-$300K. Royalty 6% + 2% ad.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-involved fast-casual; own other non-competing brands freely.

01WHAT IT IS

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH n/a LIQUID n/a
01WHAT IT IS
Hawaiian-themed mobile smoothies + coffee (carts/events); ~80-126 US units; parent Kahala Brands.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around flexible/mobile. Total investment $31,100-$200,250 (mobile carts lowest). Cart/kiosk, blenders, espresso, POS, signage. Franchise fee $30K-$50K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~80-126 US units, 48 states. Positive on low-cost/low-fee model. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Not disclosed. Mobile smoothie is high-margin, low-overhead. No separate royalty/marketing fees (unusual).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
No standard percentage royalty indicated - confirm in FDD Item 6 (some brands use flat monthly or per-unit fees instead).
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Known gate figures: NW req $750K; liquid $250K
01WHAT IT IS
Specialty bundt cake bakery; ~700-800 US units (~643 franchised in 2025 FDD); parent KKR (acquired $2B+ from Roark, March 2026).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $35K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $484-700K. ~2,000 sq ft; total investment $483,600-$907,000. Commercial ovens, mixers, cooler, decorating stations, POS, signage. Franchise fee $35K-$45K. Requires $250K liquid / $750K net worth.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~101 net new units in 2024 (record); targeting 1,000 by 2027, ~$1B system sales. Green flags. YELLOW: Jan-2025 Sunday-operating mandate dispute (Utah legislatively exempted; Harty class action active). Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$1.35M-$1.4M (FDD); top third ~$2M; EBITDA margins 20%+ (~$350K); cash-on-cash ~50% cited. Royalty 6% + 5% ad.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-involved bakery, multi-unit friendly; competing-bakery non-compete only.
CASE FILE 133▶

Aussie Pet Mobile

Pet Services · Vol II
Buy-in fee
est. $25-50K
All-in cost
$100-180K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$60K
FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $100-200K; liquid $25-50K
01WHAT IT IS
Mobile pet-grooming franchise operating from custom vans.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $100-180K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-operated mobile grooming; own other non-competing brands freely.

03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $543K-1.4M. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Supports semi-absentee via a GM; multi-brand path marketed. Competing-daycare non-compete only.

Known gate figures: NW req $150K; liquid $70K
01WHAT IT IS
Senior-living placement and eldercare-consulting franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $73-99K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS
In-home senior care (Alzheimer's/Parkinson's specialty); ~380 locations; PE-backed (Waud Capital).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $127-201K. Total $149K-$201K. Franchise fee $55,000. Small office (~1,000 sq ft).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~360+ US locations (operates 380+ franchised/corporate locations across 44 states, Canada and Australia), growing. Waud Capital Partners acquired Senior Helpers from Advocate Health on March 21, 2024 - now PE-backed. 14 closed/acquired in 2024. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
2025 Item 19: avg gross revenue $1,686,350 for units open 60+ months (n=232). Owner take-home varies (thin after ~62% labor). Royalty 5% + ~1%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-manager hires a director; semi-absentee supported; own other non-competing brands freely.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~539 US franchised units, roughly flat/slightly declining (6 closures in 2023). Founder-owned (no PE). Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Median AUV ~$1.3M gross billings; owner take-home est. $75K-$210K by scale. Lowest royalty in category (3.5% tiered + 2.5% ad ≈ 6%).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF

Senior care; 'Grandma Guarantee,' high gate.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $100-176K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

FASTSIGNS

B2B / Business Services · Vol II
Buy-in fee
est. $25-50K
All-in cost
$231-386K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
$300K
Liquid req
$80-100K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: OPEN OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $300K; liquid $80K
01WHAT IT IS
B2B sign, graphics and visual-communications centers; #1 in category on Entrepreneur Franchise 500 for a decade. Parent: Propelled Brands. ~684 US centers.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $231-386K. Total investment ~$240K-$310K; big-ticket: wide-format printers/production equipment, retail/production build-out, vinyl cutters/laminators, install vehicle, inventory.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Growing: 636 (2020) to 684 (2024); franchisee satisfaction ~82/100; only 1 litigation case in the FDD. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
2024 Item 19 average gross sales $1,111,091 (2025 FDD, 684 centers); ~17.5% net margin puts owner earnings around $194K; industry-low 5% fee burden (3% royalty + 2% ad).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
B2B, weekday hours, GM-run capable; competing-sign non-compete only.

Mature, flat/small system under Alliance Franchise Brands. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $82-221K. Total investment ~$78K-$220K (lease vs. buy equipment); digital printing/finishing/mailing equipment, build-out; franchise fee $32,500-$48,500.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Very stable; failure rate ~2% year one, ~6% by year three; #1 printing franchise 23 years running. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): 2024 US average gross sales $766,202; print shops net ~10%, so owner take-home runs ~$50K-$100K. Royalty 6%, capped.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.

B2B print, owner-managed; own other non-competing brands freely.

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $140-410K. Total investment $31K-$391K; the franchisor funds temp payroll (removes the float burden); office lease/build-out, staff; franchise fee ~$40K-$45K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Steady growth; strong franchisee satisfaction. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): first-year offices avg $999,949; offices open 60+ months avg $5,775,277. Franchisee retains ~40% of gross margin; mature offices generate six-figure-plus owner income.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Executive-owner staffing model with staff; competing-staffing non-compete only.

Spherion Staffing

~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: OPEN MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
Known gate figures: NW req $200-300K; liquid $150-350K
01WHAT IT IS
General + professional staffing; brand dates to 1946, HQ Atlanta. ~195-211 units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $212-424K. Total investment $211,725-$423,925 (2025 FDD); office build-out, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Established, stable. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average sales ~$5.6M (2024), up to $7.4M per established franchisee; avg gross profit ~$1.2M; franchisor funds temp payroll and keeps ~12-25% of gross profit.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Boutique payroll/HR/workforce management. Founded 2008, Littleton CO. ~54-55 franchised.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $77-112K. Total investment $77,375-$111,885; franchise fee $68,500-$88,500; low equipment - office, payroll software, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Steady small system (franchising since 2012). Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
No robust Item 19; third-party AUV estimate ~$190K; payroll-service nets ~20-35% (recurring, low COGS).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

BIZ-OPP HANDS-ON SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NONE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH n/a LIQUID n/a
01WHAT IT IS
Medical-billing/RCM business - a LICENSING model, not a franchise (no royalties, no territory limits). Founded 1994; ~2,000+ businesses launched.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: package price (varies). This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around low. One-time ~$26,990-$35,000 all-in; home-based; billing software, working capital, E&O insurance.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running license program; no FDD protections or unit data. Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside.
05OWNER PROFIT
No Item 19; marketing figures cite ~$25K-$50K gross per doctor client; medical billing nets 30%+.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: none / not a franchise. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Growing - 336 franchises in 2020 to 447 in 2024; 12 closures in 2024 (none under 12 months old). Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD) reports units-managed and revenue-per-unit; 2022 avg annual revenue ~$1.24M per established franchise (aggregator); property management nets ~10-20% of managed revenue; recurring.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.

Property-management model, commonly absentee-friendly; competing-PM non-compete only.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Pillar To Post

Real Estate Services / Inspection · Vol II

est. $25-50K

$44-55K
Royalty+ad
7%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: OPEN OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $44-200K; liquid $10-50K
01WHAT IT IS
Home inspection - North America's largest. Parent: FirstService Brands. ~376-550 franchises; founded 1994.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $44-55K. Total investment ~$41K-$134K; franchise fee $25K-$58,500; inspection tools/tech, vehicle, E&O, marketing; home-based.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Mixed unit trend - roughly flat to ~2% recent decline; ~5% failure rate; repeated Franchise Business Review satisfaction awards. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19: system avg sales ~$351K; top-performing multi-inspector groups avg $1,448,656; avg job size ~$745-$806; nets ~30-40% owner-operator. Royalty 7% + 4%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $113-443K. Total $1.06M-$1.84M. Franchise fee $45,000. Real-estate improvements, equipment, signage, opening reserves.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~190 US units, all franchised, steadily growing; many franchisees go multi-unit. Founder-owned. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
2024 avg gross revenue ~$460K-$518K; estimated earnings ~$55K-$69K single unit.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Lean 24/7 gym, GM-run; competing-gym non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
Reformer Pilates group studios; ~1,400 studios; parent Xponential (NYSE: XPOF).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $186-388K. Total $385K-$839K. Franchise fee $65,000. Reformer equipment + build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~1,400 studios (largest Pilates franchise). Major red flags via parent Xponential Fitness: the FTC's March 2026 settlement included $17 million returned to franchisees - the largest amount ever returned to consumers in a franchise case - and a separate $22.75 million class-action settlement over 35 months with 509 existing and former franchisees (~$40M combined). Club Pilates North American same-store sales fell 3% for full-year 2025 (Xponential systemwide SSS fell 4.3% in Q4; global studio count 3,097). ~50 active litigation cases; franchisee satisfaction ~45/100. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$966K-$984K (2025); ~15% net; owner earnings ~$145K-$174K. Royalty 7% + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Built on a semi-absentee 'executive model'; competing-Pilates non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
45-minute functional HIIT circuits; ~751 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $294-719K. Total $349K-$786K. Franchise fee $60,000. Functional equipment, A/V/timers, mandatory annual LionHeart HR-monitor purchases.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~751-753 US units. Red flags: the restated 10-K showed a $372.3 million loss over two years - $178.8 million for FY2022 and $193.5 million for FY2021 - and F45 was delisted/deregistered from the NYSE effective ~Sept 3, 2023 (the stock had peaked above $17 in Aug 2021); plus mass closures in Australia and high franchisee turnover. Green flag: 2024 AUVs hit all-time high, SSS +5.6%. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
2025 FDD median gross $407,220, avg $454,320 (n=699); top third $684K, bottom $265K. ~22% margins at scale, but $5,500/mo minimum fees create a cash trap for low-volume units.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~$3,850/yr flat. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 185▶

Facial Mania Med Spa

Health / Fitness / Wellness · Vol II
Buy-in fee
$45K
All-in cost
$312-864K
Royalty+ad
6%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
Known gate figures: NW req $400K; liquid $150K
01WHAT IT IS
Med spa; tiered/membership model.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $45K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $312-864K. The buy-in fee alone is $45K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Wax-only membership studios; ~1,067 US units; public company.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $278-425K. Total $327,600-$836,950. Franchise fee $45,000 new. 1,200-1,800 sq ft, 8-12 wax suites.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~1,062-1,067 US units. Red flag: "reset year" 2025 with 40-60 planned closures (net closures guided to 23-28); same-store sales roughly flat (+0.7% Q1 FY25). Public (NASDAQ: EWCZ). Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Mature centers (5yr+) AUV ~$1.1M; cash-on-cash returns ~40% (down from prior years); ~90% recurring membership revenue. Royalty 6% + 3% = 9% burden.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owners must be involved day-to-day (not absentee), but heavily multi-unit; non-compete covers competing waxing only.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req $100-250K; liquid $50K
01WHAT IT IS
Largest pool service/maintenance/repair/renovation franchise, Authority Brands. ~340-375 units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $84-213K. Item 7 $88,695-$213,171. Franchise fee $40K; sliding 5-7% royalty + 1% brand. Service trucks/wraps, pool equipment/tools, chemicals, working capital; home-based.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~375 units, 20+ years; system-wide sales $91M (2021, +27%). Growing. Recurring-revenue model. Green flag. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average gross revenue ~$858K; top 25% averaged $1.78M-$1.93M; best single territory net profit $518,336 (2023). Pool service net ~15-20%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5-7%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Manager-run pool-service model; competing-pool non-compete only.

Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
Known gate figures: NW req ~$175K; liquid $75-100K
01WHAT IT IS
Air duct/HVAC cleaning; 6% royalty, BELFOR backing.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: est. $25-50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $167-236K. The buy-in fee alone is est. $25-50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
