01WHAT IT IS
Residential/commercial insulation franchise, Empower Brands (MidOcean Partners PE-backed). ~395 units / 333 territories.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $49.5K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $49.5K fee + terr. Item 7 $189,075-$234,272. Franchise fee $49,500; 6.5%→4.5% tiered royalty + 2% marketing. Insulation trucks/rigs, blowing/spray equipment, materials, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Rapid growth from 2018 pilot to 400+ territories/30+ states. Growing. Green flag. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Brand-reported average revenue $952,193 with ~46.8% retained as profit (~$242,161); median gross sales ~$743K; AUV ~$1.06M (2024 data). Strong niche margins.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 203▶

DSD distribution routes

Routes / Distributorships (resale) · Vol II
Buy-in fee
route purchase price
All-in cost
$50-700K
Royalty+ad
n/a - markup on product
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
varies
ROUTE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: CONTRACT TERR MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH n/a LIQUID n/a
01WHAT IT IS
Direct-store-delivery distributorship for a branded product line in a protected territory.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: route purchase price. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $50-700K. Route purchase $20K-$350K depending on brand/volume; refrigerated routes need a reefer vehicle.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Broad, stable across many CPG brands; active resale market. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Category margins 15-30%, net $40K-$135K per route; refrigerated routes have tighter markups but higher dollars per stop.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: n/a - markup on product. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Customer demand is essentially built in - the product brand or the operating contract supplies the volume. That's what you're paying for. Your job is service and retention, not lead generation.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is defined by your route/operating CONTRACT, and may be adjustable by the counterparty. Get the boundaries in writing.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Stable; parent publicly traded; resale via general route marketplaces. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
DSD food category benchmark ~18-25% commission/margin, net $40K-$120K. High-velocity, low-stale product.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.

Territory is defined by your route/operating CONTRACT, and may be adjustable by the counterparty. Get the boundaries in writing.

All-in cost lands around varies. Service van + recharge equipment + inventory $30K-$80K; certifications/licensing required.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Recession-resistant - demand is regulatory-driven; fragmented, with consolidation opportunity. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
High-margin recurring compliance service (50%+ on service labor); net income commonly $60K-$150K owner-operator.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.

01WHAT IT IS
Home-based travel agency; tech/marketing bundled in fee, no real estate. Recurring commissions.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $10,995. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $2K-$23K. The buy-in fee alone is $10,995, with ongoing royalty/ad running 1.5-3%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 1.5-3%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

$9K-$35K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Weekly print advertising; home-based, no storefront.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: ~$9,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $9K-$35K. The buy-in fee alone is ~$9,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: OPEN OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Executive search/recruiting. Plano TX. ~170-179 franchised; founded 1959.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around under $15K. Two models - conversion $11,400-$14,800 (fee $7,500) vs. start-up $108,250-$143,580 (fee $88,000); royalty 8% + 2%; office, ATS software, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-established, stable. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
No clear avg-revenue Item 19; search firms bill 20-33% of placed salary - high margin, deal-dependent.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

FranServe

Net worth req
not published - verify FDD

LICENSE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: OPEN OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Franchise consulting/brokerage network - a licensing/biz-opp, NOT a unit franchise. Founded 2012; ~1,000-2,000 affiliated consultants.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $25K+. Training/investment ~$24,900-$50,000; minimum liquid ~$30,000; home-based.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Large consultant network; not a franchise so no FDD unit data. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
No Item 19; consultants earn referral commissions; FranServe takes 5% on the first $200K of referral fees; income highly variable.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies - license terms. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS
Medical/dental billing & practice-management - a LICENSING model, 0% royalty. Irvine CA, founded 1993; ~2,000+ businesses launched.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around low. Total ~$19,995-$55,000 depending on program; home-based; software, working capital, E&O.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running license program, not a franchise. Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside.
05OWNER PROFIT
No Item 19; sample projections ~$51,840/yr at 2 clients (not audited); high-margin, recurring once clients land.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: none / not a franchise. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Large global brand, steady. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (regional): avg collected revenue ~$192K-$206K; top single-coach ~$674K; coaching margin high. Royalty 10% (min ~$1,950/mo) + 5%.

Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.

A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS
Independent insurance agency (auto/home/commercial), Latino-market focus, 100+ carriers. Miami; founded 1980. ~200-238 units, all franchised.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $12K-$84K. Total investment $12,000-$84,000 (low); franchise fee $10K-$25K; office, licensing, E&O insurance, agency-management software, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Steady growth (80 in 2014 to 238); #1 in category on 2025 Franchise 500; no litigation disclosed. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19: AUV ~$397,894; estimated earnings ~$71,621-$99,474 (~18-25% of revenue); fast payback (1.6-3.6 years). Royalty 10-14% of commissions + ~7% marketing.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: applies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Agency model, staff-run capable; competing-insurance non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
Branded apparel/promotional-products B2B (formerly EmbroidMe). Parent: United Franchise Group. ~275-285 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $129K-$394K. Total investment ~$119K-$354K; franchise fee $49,500; embroidery/DTG equipment, heat presses, build-out, inventory.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Rebranded from EmbroidMe; mature UFG brand. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 AUV ~$331,000 per one aggregator; promo/apparel nets ~10-15%. Royalty tiered 6% down to 2% + 1%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~10%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Midas 2024 FDD Item 19 covers 886 shops operating 24+ months; yearly gross sales ~$1,034,569-$1.11M; auto repair nets ~10-15%; franchise-affiliated shops clear 3.5x-5.5x SDE at resale.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 10%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Manager-run auto-service multi-unit; competing-auto non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
Collision repair network. Parent: Driven Brands (since 2015).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: ~$20,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $298K-$804K. Can start low (~$24K franchise fee) for conversions of existing body shops; equipment-heavy if greenfield (frame machines, paint booths, spray equipment).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Large collision network. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19-cited AUV ~$2,265,000/yr (some aggregators cite up to $3.0M gross); collision nets ~5-12%; DRP insurance relationships drive the volume.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Full-service auto repair, faith-based, closed weekends. Founded 1982. ~302 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $135,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $520K-$645K. Total investment $530K-$645K; franchise fee $135,000; equipment/furniture/software $255K-$280K (lifts, bays, diagnostic equipment); franchisor handles real estate - $0 at startup, monthly rent thereafter.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Strong growth (~10.8% avg YoY sales); low SBA default (3.0%); historically near-zero closures. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): average unit revenue $2,877,457 (2024, 302 units); top quartile ~$3.2M; nets ~4.8-10%, so owner earnings run ~$100K-$180K with total owner benefit higher. Unique royalty: 50% profit split with no royalty until profitable, + up to 5% marketing.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Explicitly owner-operator, hands-on the first 12-18 months; nothing bars owning other non-competing businesses.

CASE FILE 243▶

9Round

Fitness / Boutique Studios · Vol III
Buy-in fee
n/a
All-in cost
$131K-$390K
Royalty+ad
~$1,175/mo flat
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$50K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
30-minute kickboxing circuit; ~200 US units (declining); founder-owned.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $131K-$390K. Total $149K-$416K. Franchise fee $14,900-$24,900. Boxing/kickboxing stations, ~1,200 sq ft build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Major red flag - actively contracting from 439 to ~199-200 US units; ~20%/yr closure rate vs. 2-3% industry norm. Founder-owned; non-exclusive territory. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Gross ~$141K/unit; owner earnings ~$21K-$25K (low). Royalty 6% or ~$600/mo flat + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~$1,175/mo flat. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Small-footprint gym, manager-run capable; own other non-competing brands freely.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Boxing/fitness; low-cost boutique.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $35K min. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
20-min tech-driven personal training; lower overhead.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around varies. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Premium indoor cycling; ~200 US units; now parent Extraordinary Brands.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $338K-$511K. Total $338K-$1.11M (wide by build). Franchise fee $60,000. CycleTheatre build-out, bikes, A/V.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~183-219 US units. Ownership change: divested by Xponential to Extraordinary Brands (2025) - no longer under the Xponential litigation cloud; new owner prioritizing stability over growth. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$388K-$407K; owner earnings ~$61K-$73K. Royalty 7% + local ad.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies (Xponential). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Boutique studio, manager-run; competing-cycling non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
Ballet-barre isometric group fitness; ~624 US units; parent Xponential.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $60,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $265K-$419K. Total $314K-$629K. Franchise fee $60,000. Barre-studio build-out, minimal equipment.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~599-624 US units, largest barre brand, roughly flat/modest growth (SSS +4% 2024). Same Xponential FTC/class-action cloud as Club Pilates. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$400K (up 3% YoY 2024); owner earnings ~$52K-$62K. Royalty 7% + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Semi-absentee options; own other non-competing brands freely.

F45 Training (v2)

Fitness / Boutique Studios · Vol III
Buy-in fee
n/a
All-in cost
$294K-$719K
Royalty+ad
~$3,850/yr flat
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
45-minute functional HIIT circuits; ~751 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $294K-$719K. Total $349K-$786K. Franchise fee $60,000. Functional equipment, A/V/timers, mandatory annual LionHeart HR-monitor purchases.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~751-753 US units. Red flags: the restated 10-K showed a $372.3 million loss over two years - $178.8 million for FY2022 and $193.5 million for FY2021 - and F45 was delisted/deregistered from the NYSE effective ~Sept 3, 2023 (the stock had peaked above $17 in Aug 2021); plus mass closures in Australia and high franchisee turnover. Green flag: 2024 AUVs hit all-time high, SSS +5.6%. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
2025 FDD median gross $407,220, avg $454,320 (n=699); top third $684K, bottom $265K. ~22% margins at scale, but $5,500/mo minimum fees create a cash trap for low-volume units.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~$3,850/yr flat. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Full-size "No Judgments" gym with classes/amenities; ~423 units; majority-owned by Leonard Green & Partners.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $304K-$2M+. Total $804K-$6.7M (Signature clubs cost more). 16,000-25,000 sq ft build-out, extensive equipment, HydroMassage/tanning. Royalty 5% + 2% marketing.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
423 total units (415 franchised, 8 company), growing ~+32%; sells 100+ franchises/year; ~$1.2 billion in 2024 systemwide sales. Ownership change: Leonard Green & Partners acquired majority interest from TPG Growth in a deal announced April 15, 2025. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$2.5M average; operating profit ~21.6%. Owner take-home potentially $300K-$500K+ at a mature large-format club.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
GM-run gym, multi-unit; own other non-competing brands freely.

01WHAT IT IS
Heart-rate-based interval group training; ~1,290 US units; parent Purpose Brands.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around varies. Total $822K-$1.4M. Heart-rate tech, treadmills/rowers/weights, premium A/V, build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~1,283-1,298 US franchised units, contracting from peak. Merged into Purpose Brands (with Anytime Fitness) in 2024. No exclusive territory; franchisee-satisfaction concerns cited. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Median AUV ~$1.0M (n=1,269 US); ~16.5% net; owner earnings ~$121K-$145K. Royalty 8% + 2% (high burden).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owners are expected to manage directly (not absentee); favors multi-unit developers. Own other non-competing brands freely.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 259▶

V's Barbershop

Beauty / Salon / Barber / Lash / Wax · Vol III
Buy-in fee
$30,000
All-in cost
$316K-$592K
Royalty+ad
3.5-5%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Upscale barbershop.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $316K-$592K. The buy-in fee alone is $30,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 3.5-5%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 3.5-5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Eyelash-extension + brow membership studios; ~130 units; parent WellBiz Brands.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $59,900. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $286K-$449K. Total $286K-$461K. Franchise fee $59,900. ~750 sq ft build-out; licensed stylists.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~128-130 US franchised, modest growth. Parent WellBiz Brands; long payback ~9-11 years. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
2024 Item 19 avg gross $313,480 (n=109). Royalty 6% + 3% + $2K/mo local marketing.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Membership lash studio, manager-run; competing-lash non-compete only.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 271▶

Sola Salon Studios

Beauty / Salon / Barber / Lash / Wax · Vol III
Buy-in fee
$55,000
All-in cost
$545K-$1.74M
Royalty+ad
5.5%
Net worth req
$500K
Liquid req
$150K
FRANCHISE ABSENTEE-OK SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Salon-suite rental to independent beauty pros; 729 units; category leader.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $55,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $545K-$1.74M. Total $1.18M-$1.94M - driven almost entirely by leasehold improvements (build private suites in 3,000-9,000 sq ft).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
729 total units (660 franchised + 69 company), category leader, steady growth (+29-46 franchised/yr). Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
2024 Item 19 avg gross $442,438, median $420,052 (n=625); mature occupancy 84.7%; median EBITDA ~$191,547 cited. Landlord model (lease suites to stylists). Royalty 5.5% + 1.5%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Salon-suite 'landlord' model; semi-absentee, own other businesses freely.

Buy-in fee: $52,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~399 franchised units, growing +8.7%. Celebrity-founder marketing. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Median AUV ~$426K (n~134); ~17.5% net; owner earnings ~$64K-$85K. Very low royalty (per-square-foot / ~0.3% effective).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Salon-suite 'landlord' model; semi-absentee, competing-suite non-compete only.

n/a

cash $250K
Royalty+ad
6%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Facial bar.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around cash $250K. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

All-in cost lands around $76K-$94K. The buy-in fee alone is $39K-$40K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 7.5% + 1.5%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF

Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

All-in cost lands around $337K-$685K. The buy-in fee alone is $35,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. 10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Kumon

Tutoring / Music / Arts / Kids · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$2,000
All-in cost
$73K-$165K
Royalty+ad
$36/student/mo
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$70K
FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
After-school math & reading self-learning centers. Parent: Kumon Institute of Education (Japan). ~1,689 US units; 27,000+ centers in 60+ countries.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $2,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $73K-$165K. Total investment $74K-$166K (among the most affordable education franchises); franchise fee $1,000-$2,000; modest center build-out; all materials bought from Kumon.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Red flag: ~20% annual closure/churn; no exclusive territory; short 5-yr term. But near-zero SBA default (~0.5%). Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 AUV ~$173K (median across ~1,689 US units); ~30% net margin, so owner earnings run ~$52K-$70K. Per-student royalty (~$30-$38/student/mo) scales with enrollment.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: $36/student/mo. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
No absentee owners - the franchisee must personally run sessions full-time - but no bar on unrelated ownership.

Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Math-only tutoring/learning centers. Parent: Roark Capital Group (acquired 2021). 1,256 total locations globally; founded 2002.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $49,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $113K-$150K. Total investment $113K-$150K; franchise fee ~$49K; center build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Record 2025 - 88 new locations worldwide and 121 new franchise agreements. 12% total fee burden (10% royalty + 2% ad). Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD, 853 US centers): average annual Gross Receipts $293,590; top-50% average $380,553; owner EBITDA $35,231-$44,039 (19-23% margin). Multi-center owners average higher ($419,845 gross for 6+ centers).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 10% + $650/mo. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Center-director-run once established; own other non-competing brands freely.

$89K-$328K
Royalty+ad
5%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
Boba tea + boba-supply importer/manufacturer; ~23 franchised units; parent Boba Loca.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $10K-$14K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $89K-$328K. Total investment ~$88,945-$327,955. Tea brewing/sealing equipment, POS, signage. Franchise fee $10K-$14K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~23 units; thin public data. Higher risk - recommend direct FDD pull. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Not disclosed. Boba kiosks net ~10-20%. Royalty 5%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Boba Nation

Food & Bev - Boba / Juice / Coffee · Vol III
Buy-in fee
$30,000
All-in cost
$350K-$496K
Royalty+ad
5% + 1%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Multi-beverage/dessert.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $350K-$496K. The buy-in fee alone is $30,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 5% + 1%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 1%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Nutrition-focused smoothies (+ "Power Eats" food menu 2026); ~1,242 US units (1,149 franchised / 52 company); parent Smoothie King (private).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $312K-$1.38M. Total investment $346,000-$1,278,000. Blenders, prep, POS, signage; ovens added 2026 (franchisor-funded). Franchise fee $30K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
74 net openings in 2025 (record); ~98% 5-year survival; 0.74% SBA default rate. Green flags. Competition from Tropical Smoothie. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
System AUV ~$627,000; top-half ~$830,000; bottom-quartile ~$410,000 (FDD Item 19). Smoothie nets ~10-15%. Royalty 6% + 3% (9% total).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner/manager-run smoothie QSR; competing-smoothie non-compete only.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $39,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around mid. Total investment $470,000-$759,500. Espresso machines, waffle irons, prep, POS, signage, seating. Franchise fee $39K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~30-60 units, growing; build costs rising. Neutral-positive. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
AUV ~$613,000-$614,229 (FDD). Coffee+food cafe nets ~10-18%. Royalty 6% + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 323▶

Chester's Chicken

Food & Bev - QSR / Fast-Casual · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
low
All-in cost
$28K-$297K
Royalty+ad
varies - license terms
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
LICENSE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
QSR chicken (c-store/kiosk); very low-end entry. No experience.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: low. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $28K-$297K. The buy-in fee alone is low, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies - license terms. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies - license terms. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

All-in cost lands around $109K-$751K. Traditional $425,800-$820,500; virtual kitchen $142,000-$321,000. Fryers, hood, POS, signage. Franchise fee $30K-$40K.

RED FLAG - severe contraction from ~100+ units; small franchised base; rebuild under new owner. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.

2021 avg gross sales ~$1.01M on a very small sample (24 franchised units open all year); another cite $710,452. Wings QSR nets ~6-10%. Royalty 6% + ~4% ad.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Wings/tenders QSR, asset-light small-box model; ~2,563 units worldwide, ~1,988+ US franchised; parent Wingstop Inc. (NASDAQ: WING).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $20,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $259K-$912K. 1,200-2,000 sq ft inline; total investment $259,400-$912,100. Fryers, hood, POS, signage, inventory, working capital. No dine-in complexity = lower build.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
349 net new units in 2024 (+15.8% to 2,563 worldwide); $4.8B system sales; 21st consecutive year of same-store-sales growth. Strong green flags. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Per Wingstop's Q4/FY2024 results, US same-store sales rose ~20%, ending 2024 with an AUV of $2.1M (up from $1.8M in 2023). Small-box, low-labor model; cash-on-cash returns among the best in QSR given low build cost. Royalty 6% + ~5% ad.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5-6% + 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
No absentee ownership; single-unit owners must live in-market. Non-compete blocks competing wing concepts only.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Fast-casual hand-breaded chicken tenders/wings + Southern sides; ~300 marketed (200+ in FDD-captured base, 11 company), 1,000+ in development; Roark-affiliated growth.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around high. Full kitchen + often drive-thru; total investment $1,228,900-$4,466,000. Full hood/fryer line for hand-breading, drive-thru, dine-in FF&E, POS, signage. Franchise fee $30K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~70% restaurant growth over 3 years; ~40% same-store-sales growth over 4 years; >$569M system sales. Green flag on growth; caution on build-cost-to-AUV ratio. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Per QSR Research Hub's analysis of the 2025 FDD Item 19, the system average across 147 qualifying locations is $2.44M; the top quartile averaged $3.80M and the best unit generated $5.36M in 2024. Caution: system-average payback is long (~9 years) at a ~$2.85M midpoint build - the model works at top-quartile execution. Royalty 6% + 2% marketing (8% total).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + ad. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-operator chicken QSR; competing-concept non-compete only.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Multi-unit QSR commitment; nothing bars owning unrelated franchises.
CASE FILE 332▶

Touching Hearts at Home

Senior Care / Home Care (new brands) · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$39,500
All-in cost
$53K-$76K
Royalty+ad
~5-6% + 1-2% (est.)
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Non-medical senior care; office + marketing + insurance. NW $100K, liquid $50K.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $39,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $53K-$76K. The buy-in fee alone is $39,500, with ongoing royalty/ad running ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: ~5-6% + 1-2% (est.). This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $50,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $92K-$165K. Total $92K-$165K (lowest entry). Franchise fee $49,500 (25% vet discount).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~486-551 US franchised offices, growing. Green flag: FDD discloses NO Item 3 litigation (cleaner than peers). HQ Omaha. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
2025 Item 19 avg net billings ~$1.34M; owner take-home varies. Royalty 5% + 2% (first $1M).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Care-director-run; own other non-competing brands freely.
