01WHAT IT IS
Non-medical home care via registry model; ~170-200 units; first US home-care franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around moderate. Total $99K-$181K. Franchise fee $49,500 (20% vet/first-responder discount). Office required.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~170-200 locations in 30-32 states, steady/modest growth. First US non-medical home-care franchise (since 1984). HQ Blue Bell, PA. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Company markets avg gross receipts ~$1.9M-$2.13M; median ~$1.7M; gross margin ~49.9%. Lowest royalty in senior care (4% + 0.5%).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Care-manager-run; own other non-competing brands freely.

FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Senior-living placement; home-based, no staff.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around cash $50K. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS
Carpet/upholstery/tile/duct cleaning franchise; family-owned since 1947. ~267-290 units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: ~$20,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around -. Item 7 $147K-$522K. Truck-mounted equipment (expensive), fleet, warehouse/office, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~267 US units; stable. 7% core royalty + 2% marketing. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2023 data) average gross sales ~$1.73M (highest AUV in carpet cleaning); net ~12-15% → ~$150K-$190K est. earnings.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7% + 4%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Manager-run cleaning; competing-cleaning non-compete only.

~2,200+ US units; grew ~12% over 3 years. #1 in restoration category 20+ years. Green flag = scale; red flag = no FPR transparency + short 5-year term. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.

No Item 19 disclosed. Third-party estimated AUV ~$1.69M; residential restoration sub-sector averages ~$911K gross. Restoration net ~13-20%.

Royalty+ad
4% + 0.5%
Net worth req
$300K
Liquid req
$100K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Fire/water/mold restoration + reconstruction franchise, FirstService Brands. ~355-370 US units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $61K-$123K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $190K-$511K. Item 7 $298,800-$804,900 (higher-end, includes reconstruction). Franchise fee $55K. Equipment fleet, box trucks, warehouse, larger working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~370 locations; brand system sales rose 5.7% to $1.49B (2024). Growing. Strong FPR transparency. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (per IFA/brand listing) - "2+ year-old franchises average $6,006,779 in gross sales" and "Under 2-year-old franchise average $1,765,113 in gross sales"; median AUV ~$4.0M across 355 US locations. Restoration net ~13.5% → ~$477K-$596K owner earnings at median.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 4% + 0.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Team-based restoration, owner-led; competing-business non-compete only.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Water, fire and mold restoration franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $29K-$49K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $70K-$227K. The buy-in fee alone is $29K-$49K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 10% + 1%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 10% + 1%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Buy-in fee: $39,900. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $135K-$171K. Item 7 $167K-$258K. Royalty 8% + 1% ad. Drying/extraction equipment, 1-2 vans, working capital; can start home-based.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~95 units across ~99 markets; steady. Handles 20,000+ claims/yr. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.

01WHAT IT IS

Buy-in fee: $39,900. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $85K-$150K. Item 7 $129K-$142K. Office, vehicles, ladders/equipment, staff, working capital. No franchise experience needed.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~264 outlets; stable ~255-270 with low single-digit closures. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average sales ~$489K (2023 data); ~$441K (2022). Window-cleaning net ~15-20%. (Note: a "$1.6M average" cited elsewhere conflates the company-owned St. Louis unit - use $441K-$489K.)
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + $50/mo. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. 04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES

Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

MyWay Mobile Storage

Junk / Moving / Storage (new) · Vol III
Buy-in fee
$40K+
All-in cost
$179K-$760K
Royalty+ad
3%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE ABSENTEE-OK SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Portable storage container business. Smaller system.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40K+. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $179K-$760K. Containers, trucks, warehouse; ~$500K-$1M.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Smaller system. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Container-rental economics; owner income $75K-$250K at scale.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 3%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Semi-absentee / manager-run, and the non-compete covers competing businesses only - it slots cleanly into a multi-brand portfolio.

01WHAT IT IS
Self-storage facility development franchise - real-estate-heavy.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $39K-$69K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $500K-$4M. Multimillion-dollar facility development (land + construction).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Newer self-storage-development franchise. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Income $100K-$400K+ once a facility stabilizes; this is a real-estate development play, not an operating business at first.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + 2.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Self-storage, semi-absentee investor model; competing-storage non-compete only.
CASE FILE 380▶

Surface Specialists Systems

Home Improvement - Kitchen/Bath/Closet/Floor · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
n/a
All-in cost
$43K-$56K
Royalty+ad
flat monthly
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Bath/kitchen surface refinishing; liquid $25K.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $43K-$56K. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running flat monthly. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: flat monthly. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Largest interior remodeler in the US; growing. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Brand reports systemwide sales $494M (2024) and franchisee average gross profit margin 51.3% (2024, vs. 24.9% industry average). Net owner margin lower after overhead. High revenue per unit.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Remodeling model, manager-run capable; competing-business non-compete only.

Closets by Design

Home Improvement - Kitchen/Bath/Closet/Floor · Vol III
Buy-in fee

All-in cost
$154K-$511K
Royalty+ad
7.25% + 2.25%
Net worth req
$200K
Liquid req
$100K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
Custom closet and home-organization franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $46,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $154K-$511K. The buy-in fee alone is $46,500, with ongoing royalty/ad running 7.25% + 2.25%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7.25% + 2.25%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Mobile window-coverings sales/installation (blinds, shades, shutters). Parent: Home Franchise Concepts (Empower Brands). ~1,355-1,489 US units - the largest window-coverings franchise; founded 1992.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $20K + $70K terr. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $140K-$212K. Total investment $100,500-$211,250; franchise fee $19,950 + territory fee; royalty 3.5% + ~2% ad; van-based, no store build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~97% 3-year continuity; 0% SBA default. Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025/2026 FDD): single-territory average gross sales ~$853,650 (2024); median AUV ~$540K; top-third ~$1.05M+; multi-territory (3+) ~$2.5M+; nets 15-20%, so owner income runs ~$75K-$90K single territory.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: flat. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 406▶

Augusta Lawn Care

Lawn / Landscape / Irrigation (new) · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$15K-$25K
All-in cost
$21K-$83K
Royalty+ad
9%
Net worth req
$75K
Liquid req
$30K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Lawn-maintenance franchise with a low-cost entry.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $15K-$25K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $21K-$83K. The buy-in fee alone is $15K-$25K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 9%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 9%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Low-cost lawn model, manager-run capable; own other non-competing brands freely.

Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 3.5-6.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 417▶

Naturals2Go

Vending / Ice / Micro-Markets · Vol III CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$26,000
All-in cost
$50K-$250K
Royalty+ad
none
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
~$50K
BIZ-OPP ABSENTEE-OK SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NONE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Healthy-snack/drink vending machine business-opportunity program with locating services - NOT a franchise (no royalties, no FDD).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $26,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $50K-$250K. Multi-machine packages commonly $10K-$100K+.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running healthy-vending provider; mixed operator reviews industrywide. Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside.
05OWNER PROFIT
Vending economics apply: $200-$600 net/machine/mo at good sites; profitability is almost entirely location quality.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
No standard percentage royalty indicated - confirm in FDD Item 6 (some brands use flat monthly or per-unit fees instead).
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Semi-absentee / manager-run, and the non-compete covers competing businesses only - it slots cleanly into a multi-brand portfolio.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
IV vitamin/hydration therapy (cash-pay); ~106 units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around cash $150K. Total $147K-$415K. Franchise fee $50K-$55K. 1,000-1,500 sq ft; requires licensed medical director/nurses.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~106 US operating units ("600+ in development" is aspirational). Early-stage, rapid growth. Founder/CEO-owned. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Limited disclosure; third-party avg revenue ~$348K-$571K; owner earnings ~$52K-$70K. Royalty 7% + 1-2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 8%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Cash-pay IV model, manager-run capable; competing-IV non-compete only.

05OWNER PROFIT
Company-location avg gross ~$974K (2026 FDD range $1.06M-$2.33M for 5 company sites). Membership model. Royalty 5%.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $750K-$1M. The buy-in fee alone is n/a, with ongoing royalty/ad running applies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Net worth req

FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Dance-fitness class franchise. Parent: Jazzercise, Inc.; since 1969, franchising 1983.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $1,250. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $2.2K-$2.8K. Low investment (~$3K-$35K); can use rented space (churches, community centers).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Mature - declined from peak but a stable core remains. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Instructor-owner income is modest ($30K-$80K) unless running multi-class/studio operations; low royalty burden.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 20% of class revenue. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Sign Gypsies

Shredding & Niche Low-Cost · Vol III CLEARS

verify FDD
LICENSE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Celebration yard-sign (greeting) rental home-based business. Large home-based network.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: ~$1,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around low. Sign inventory; very low investment (~$3K-$10K).
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Large "yard card" network; low barrier to entry. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner income modest ($15K-$50K, often part-time).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies - license terms. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Large, mature; the kiosk model lowers entry cost; stable low-growth industry. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 439▶

Mosquito Shield

Pest / Mosquito / Wildlife · Vol IV CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$30,000
All-in cost
$80K-$132K
Royalty+ad
9% + 1%
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$50K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Mosquito and tick control franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $80K-$132K. The buy-in fee alone is $30,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 9% + 1%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 9% + 1%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Recurring pest model, semi-absentee capable; own other non-competing brands freely.

Liquid req
varies
DEALER GM-ABLE SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NEGOTIATED MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Established pest brand; conversion/dealer options.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around dealer/varies. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is NEGOTIATED as a dealer - there may be no formal protection at all. Insist on written terms.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $80K-$180K. The buy-in fee alone is $40,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 451▶

Kwik Kerb

Lawn / Deck / Fence / Concrete (new) · Vol IV CAUTION
Buy-in fee
varies
All-in cost
$25K-$60K
Royalty+ad
none
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
varies
BIZ-OPP GM-ABLE SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NONE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
CAUTION: Continuous concrete curbing; non-FDD business-opportunity seller - verify hard.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $25K-$60K. The buy-in fee alone is varies and there is no standard percentage royalty. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
No standard percentage royalty indicated - confirm in FDD Item 6 (some brands use flat monthly or per-unit fees instead).
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Plumbing service franchise (Authority Brands) known for punctuality guarantees.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $42,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $95K-$253K. Item 7 ~$91K-$201K historically (higher in later FDDs). Service vans + wraps ($40K-$55K each), tools/inventory, uniforms, dispatch/CRM software, insurance, local marketing (8% of revenue if performance minimums missed), working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
87 franchisees operating 324 territories as of Dec 31, 2023; grew from 78 franchisees/241 territories (2021). Growing. Ranked #288 Entrepreneur Franchise 500 (2025). Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (April 3, 2025 FDD, Table 2) average gross revenue $2,881,095 per franchisee (not per territory); a 2022 FDD reported $3,016,685 with 19.9% same-store sales growth. Plumbing net margins ~10-15% → owner take-home roughly $290K-$430K at average volume (before debt service).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Semi-absentee/manager-run plumbing brand; competing-trade non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS
Grout/tile/caulk restoration; owner-operator, very low overhead.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $30K-$70K. The buy-in fee alone is $25,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 5%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Buy-in fee: $54,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $99K-$140K. Item 7 ~$130K-$189K; franchise fee $19,950. Showroom/samples optional, van, materials, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~290 company+franchise units; established (since 1988). Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break.
05OWNER PROFIT
Remodeling category; publishes FPR - verify AUV (mature refacing units ~$500K-$800K); net ~15%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-driven sales early, manager-run later; competing-remodel non-compete only.

DEALER GM-ABLE SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NEGOTIATED MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
Foundation repair and stabilization franchise/dealer network.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around dealer/varies. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is NEGOTIATED as a dealer - there may be no formal protection at all. Insist on written terms.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is NEGOTIATED as a dealer - there may be no formal protection at all. Insist on written terms.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Rolling Suds

Pressure Wash / Roof / Exterior (new) · Vol IV CAUTION
Buy-in fee
$50,000
All-in cost
$200K-$450K
Royalty+ad
6% + 2%
Net worth req
$200K
Liquid req
$100K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
Power-washing franchise.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $50,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $200K-$450K. The buy-in fee alone is $50,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running 6% + 2%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $10,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $15K-$40K. The buy-in fee alone is $10,000, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Mobile and dedicated security patrol services for commercial/residential clients (rebranded "Signal").
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $52K-$220K. Patrol vehicles, uniforms, licensing; total investment commonly ~$60K-$220K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Large, growing security franchise; recurring contract revenue is the asset. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Recurring B2B contracts; category owner income $75K-$250K+ at scale; labor-heavy.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 7%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 487▶

ADT Authorized Dealer

Locksmith / Security / Safety · Vol IV CLEARS
Buy-in fee
none
All-in cost
low start
Royalty+ad
varies
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
varies
DEALER GM-ABLE SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NEGOTIATED MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Alarm/smart-home dealer; installs/monitoring - dealer program, not FDD.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: none. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around low start. The buy-in fee alone is none, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is NEGOTIATED as a dealer - there may be no formal protection at all. Insist on written terms.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

01WHAT IT IS
Direct-access lab/drug/DNA testing (B2B + B2C); ~128-143 units; parent CRESSO Brands.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $44,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $150K-$250K. Total $166K-$310K. Franchise fee $54,500. Lab draw-station build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~128-143 US units, growing (+21 over two years). Merged with Any Lab Test Now (2024) → CRESSO Brands. HQ Greenville, SC. Key risk: wide unit-economics spread. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 avg gross ~$233K, median ~$206K (very wide spread - bottom-20% median $25,694, top 25% >$750K). Royalty 7-8% + 2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 8%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.

CASE FILE 492▶

The UPS Store

Mailbox / Print / IT / Cell Repair · Vol IV
Buy-in fee
$30,000
All-in cost
$180K-$500K
Royalty+ad
5% + 2.5%
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$75K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Retail shipping, printing, mailbox and business services. ~5,234 US units; ~99% franchised - one of the largest systems anywhere.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $30,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $180K-$500K. Total investment ~$209K-$609K; retail build-out, printing/copying equipment, signage, inventory, working capital.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Large, mature, still growing (100+ net units in 2024); 99% five-year survival; ~0% SBA default. Headwind: UPS reducing Amazon volume; digital shift pressures shipping/print. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): median adjusted gross sales $686,743; average ~$721K; nets ~12-16%, putting owner earnings around $82K-$110K; top quartile ~$950K. Fee burden 7.5% (5% royalty + 2.5% ad).
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 2.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Active hands-on ownership with an on-site primary operator; competing-business non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $55,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $105K-$160K. Total investment ~$109K-$145K; franchise fee, RMM/monitoring tools/laptops, marketing launch, working capital, technician payroll; minimal real estate.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Strong growth: $123M (2021) to $206M (2024) network sales; low closures (3-4/yr). Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): 161 qualified franchisees (262 businesses) generated $206M network sales - per-business average ~$650K-$1.0M; MSP nets ~10-20% at maturity; recurring contracts $1,500-$5,000/mo per client. Royalty 7% + 1.2%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 4-7%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Executive-owner IT model; own other non-competing brands freely.

01WHAT IT IS
MSP for SMBs; ~30-year track record, largest IT franchise system in North America. ~255 units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $49,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $100K-$175K. Total investment $106,450-$159,450; franchise fee $49,950-$54,950; monitoring tools, working capital, sales/marketing; minimal real estate.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-established, broad coverage; ~2% recent growth. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 disclosed; MSP economics run ~10-20% net at maturity on recurring contracts. Royalty 6%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Executive-owner IT model; competing-IT non-compete only.
