Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
~263 clinics, explosive growth (1 in 2015 → 206 in 2024 → 263 by Oct 2025; 700+ committed); zero terminations/closures disclosed. PE-backed (Princeton Equity). Caveat: unproven mature unit economics. FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID ~
01WHAT IT IS
Halotherapy/float-tank wellness; niche, build-out.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $200K-$500K. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Youth sports leagues, camps, clinics (flag football, soccer, basketball) for ages 3-14. ~264 units, all franchised; founded 2002.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $24,900. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $60K-$85K. Total investment $59,900-$69,900 (very low); franchise fee $24,900; no real estate or build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Growing: 199 to 224 to 264 over three years. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 registration revenue ~$596K-$613K for multi-venue full-time units (a subset that skews high); single-unit figures ~$360K-$486K; solid net given no owned facility - uses existing fields/gyms.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 8% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Learn-to-swim/water-survival lessons using host commercial pools (hotels, gyms). Parent: Buzz Franchise Brands. 258 franchised outlets, 32 states; founded 1981, franchising since 2011.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $95K-$130K. Total investment ~$95K-$168K (no pool build); franchise fee $39,500-$59,500; royalty 10% + 2%.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Strongly growing - +65 outlets in 2024. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 average gross revenue ~$461K-$508K, median ~$335K; mature (pre-2022) outlets ~$580K; asset-light host-pool model means higher margins than build-a-pool concepts.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 8% + 2%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?

Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Enrichment model, staff-run capable; competing-business non-compete only.
CASE FILE 613▶

Chess Wizards

Kids - Sports / Swim / Music / Martial Arts · Vol IV CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$20,000
All-in cost
$25K-$60K
Royalty+ad
varies
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Chess-enrichment classes/camps franchise for kids. Small system.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $20,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $25K-$60K. Very low (~$25K-$60K); instructors, curriculum; no facility.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Small niche system. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Low-cost enrichment; owner earnings $30K-$80K owner-operator.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.
CASE FILE 614▶

X-Golf

Entertainment - Golf / Trampoline / Axe · Vol IV
Buy-in fee
$50,000
All-in cost
$1.2M-$2M
Royalty+ad
6%
Net worth req
$1M
Liquid req
$300K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Indoor golf-simulator "eatertainment" venues with bar/food. Parent: X Golf America (Dale Ventures majority). ~120-138 US locations; founded 2005, franchising since 2015.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $50,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $1.2M-$2M. Total investment ~$994K-$1.94M; franchise fee $35K-$40K; royalty 7% + 1% marketing; simulator bays + bar/restaurant build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Growing fast (24 units 2021 to 70+ in 2023); FDD discloses lawsuits. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 CONFLICTING - US figures cited ~$113K average (likely partial-year for a young franchisor) vs. analyst per-venue estimates of $900K-$1.2M gross at 19-23% EBITDA. Treat the US Item 19 cautiously; mature full venues clearly run higher.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Entertainment venue, manager-run capable; competing-business non-compete only.

01WHAT IT IS

02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $40,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $250K-$500K. Total investment ~$125K-$479K; franchise fee $17.5K-$35K; royalty 5% + up to 4% marketing.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Weak franchise comparison - few franchised units. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
No meaningful Item 19; largely corporate-operated; golf-instruction category AUV ~$400K-$800K.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Item 19 status ambiguous across sources - cited ~$796K-$1.0M gross (verify in the live FDD); revenue from memberships + lessons + cage rental + retail.

03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $1M-$5M. Total investment ~$3.1M-$5.8M (2.0 model up to $6.3M); franchise fee $100K; royalty 6% + 2% marketing; large facility build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Growing (+37 opened in FY2024); #1 park/entertainment franchise on Entrepreneur for 5 consecutive years. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 (2025 FDD): 2.0 Parks average gross sales ~$3M; estimated earnings ~$427K-$549K per aggregators; top-quartile operating profit ~30%.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.

Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.

Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.
CASE FILE 626▶

Painting with a Twist

Paint & Sip / DIY / Creative Studios · Vol IV
Buy-in fee
$25,000
All-in cost
$115K-$185K
Royalty+ad
6% + varies
Net worth req
$150K
Liquid req
$75K
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL MIXED ~60-75% TERR: RADIUS MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
BYOB "paint and sip" social art studios - the largest paint-and-sip franchise (~220-300+ studios). Founded 2007; acquired Bottle & Bottega (2018).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $115K-$185K. Total investment $119K-$261K; franchise fee $25K; royalty 6% + 2% marketing; studio build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category leader, franchisee-led ownership. Category survival is MIXED (~60-75%) - thinner margins and higher churn; validate unit trends carefully.
05OWNER PROFIT
Item 19 figures range ~$228K (one aggregator) to ~$622K (higher-performing base); estimated owner earnings ~$32K-$41K owner-operator; royalty does not apply to alcohol sales.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6% + varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

CASE FILE 629▶

Maid Right

Cleaning - Residential / Specialty (new) · Vol IV CLEARS
Buy-in fee
$25K-$50K
All-in cost
$50K-$130K
Royalty+ad
5% + 1%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL HIGH ~85%+ TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Residential cleaning (Premium Service Brands); recurring, home-based ops.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25K-$50K. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $50K-$130K. The buy-in fee alone is $25K-$50K, with ongoing royalty/ad running 5% + 1%. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run HIGH (~85%+) - low fixed overhead means bad years bend rather than break. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5% + 1%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
LICENSE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Backyard-chicken rental; seasonal niche, very low entry.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around low license. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%). No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $30K-$50K. Photography equipment; low investment (~$25K-$70K); home-based.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running niche franchise. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Low-cost mobile photography; owner income $40K-$90K.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Generally OPEN / non-exclusive - no territory protection; you compete with other operators of the same brand.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

Drone photo/inspection services

Buy-in fee
varies

varies
Royalty+ad
varies
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
varies
SELF-START HANDS-ON SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NONE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Drone photography/roof-inspection; niche, tech-forward.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around varies. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running varies. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: varies. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

$50K-$150K
Royalty+ad
6%
Net worth req
not published - verify FDD
Liquid req
verify FDD
FRANCHISE GM-ABLE SURVIVAL GOOD ~75-85% TERR: EXCLUSIVE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH ~ LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
Interior plantscaping design and maintenance for offices/commercial spaces - recurring B2B.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $25,000. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $50K-$150K. Vehicle + plant inventory + greenhouse arrangement; total investment commonly ~$50K-$150K.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running interior-landscaping franchise; stable B2B niche. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
Recurring service contracts; owner income commonly $50K-$150K.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 6%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

01WHAT IT IS
Custom awards/trophies/plaques retail. ~129-148 franchised units.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: $37,500. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $100K-$150K. Total investment ~$168K-$199K; all-in initial fees ~$118K-$128K incl. buildout; laser engravers/sublimation equipment, inventory, retail build-out.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Established (franchising ~1987); stable. Category survival tends to run GOOD (~75-85%).
05OWNER PROFIT
No Item 19; retail engraving/awards nets ~10-15% with modest single-unit income.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Owner-run for roughly years 1-3, then realistically delegable to a general manager. This is the fit for a portfolio/hands-off endgame.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: 5.5%. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Most franchisors give you the tools but you still bring the customers: a branded website, some national/ brand advertising funded by the ad fee, marketing templates, and training. Local lead generation - your own ad spend, reviews, networking and referrals - is on you. Confirm whether there's any real lead-assignment or call-center support (most don't have it) and what the required local marketing minimum is.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Usually only a protected RADIUS around your location - the brand can place other units in the same metro and compete for the same customers.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Owner-run early then delegable to a manager; the non-compete covers competing businesses only, so you can hold it alongside other unrelated brands.

Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Territory is NEGOTIATED as a dealer - there may be no formal protection at all. Insist on written terms.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.
CASE FILE 651▶

Self-serve car wash (resale)

Laundromat / Car Wash (dev programs) · Vol IV
Buy-in fee
n/a
All-in cost
$500K-$2M
Royalty+ad
n/a
Net worth req
n/a - not a franchise
Liquid req
varies
RESALE ABSENTEE-OK SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: NONE MULTI-BRAND OK NET WORTH NO LIQUID NO
01WHAT IT IS
Existing bays/tunnel resale; real-estate + equipment.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: n/a. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $500K-$2M. The buy-in fee alone is n/a and there is no standard percentage royalty. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Semi-passive or manager-run from early on. One of the more genuinely absentee-friendly models here - though a good manager is still everything.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
No standard percentage royalty indicated - confirm in FDD Item 6 (some brands use flat monthly or per-unit fees instead).
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.

not published - verify FDD

verify FDD
LICENSE HANDS-ON SURVIVAL UNKNOWN - no FDD TERR: EXCLUSIVE OWN OK · HANDS-ON NET WORTH YES LIQUID YES
01WHAT IT IS
CAUTION: Delivery-only brand licenses; high churn, platform-dependent.
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $10K-$100K. The buy-in fee alone is varies, with ongoing royalty/ad running % of sales. Everything between the fee and the all-in number is what the franchisor does NOT cover - equipment, vehicle, any build-out, signage, tech, licensing, insurance, training travel, and roughly three months of working capital. Pull FDD Item 7 for the exact line-item table.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside. No reliable per-brand success rate is published anywhere; the real number is FDD Item 20 - count units opened versus closed/transferred over three years and do that math before signing.
05OWNER PROFIT
Owner take-home depends on scale and how much you run yourself. Ask for FDD Item 19 (the earnings disclosure) and call current and former operators - never trust a headline average.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
Ongoing: % of sales. This comes off the top of gross sales, permanently. Confirm current rates in FDD Item 6.
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
Typically grants an EXCLUSIVE, protected territory - but confirm the size in FDD Item 12. A city can hold several territories, so 'exclusive' is not always one-per-city.
10OWN OTHER FRANCHISES / BUSINESSES?
A franchise's non-compete blocks only competing businesses, so you can own other unrelated brands - but this one is hands-on, so each needs your daily time.

01WHAT IT IS
Healthy-snack/drink vending machine business-opportunity program with locating services - NOT a franchise (no royalties, no FDD).
02INITIAL FEE
Buy-in fee: varies. This is the franchise/license fee only (FDD Item 5) - not the full cost to open.
03COSTS BEYOND THE FEE
All-in cost lands around $25K-$150K. Multi-machine packages commonly $10K-$100K+.
04TRACK RECORD + SURVIVAL
Long-running healthy-vending provider; mixed operator reviews industrywide. Survival is UNKNOWN - no FDD means no Item 20 unit data exists. You can't measure the failure rate from outside.
05OWNER PROFIT
Vending economics apply: $200-$600 net/machine/mo at good sites; profitability is almost entirely location quality.
06STAFFING / HANDS-OFF
Expect to BE the operator. This stays a job you own unless you scale to multiple units/routes and layer in managers. Not a passive play.
07ROYALTY + AD FEES
No standard percentage royalty indicated - confirm in FDD Item 6 (some brands use flat monthly or per-unit fees instead).
08CUSTOMER ACQUISITION
Little to no consumer marketing engine comes with this - you generate demand yourself. Treat customer acquisition like a self-start: your website/SEO, local ads, and relationships do the work.
09MULTIPLE OPERATORS IN ONE CITY?
NO territory protection. Others can operate right beside you.

Not a traditional franchise - no franchise covenant restricts owning other businesses.

Franchise Case Files - 656 buy-in businesses - compiled June 2026.
Deep-dive sections lean on FDD item references where brand-specific research is limited. All figures are screening estimates; verify against live FDDs and validation calls. Not financial, legal, or tax advice.
